data.output:
1	# Artifact Generalization & Three-Layer Run Architecture
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Approved (refined through 3 review rounds with codex + fresh-eyes)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Two threads converge in this plan:
9	
10	1. **Generalize artifacts beyond markdown documents.** Diagrams, wireframes, hero images, prompts — text and binary — become first-class artifacts via media-type plurality and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files. Generated artifacts are checked into git; regeneration is source-hash-driven.
11	2. **Three-layer run architecture.** DDx owns three explicit layered primitives: `ddx run` (single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt in isolated worktree), `ddx work` (queue drain). The current two-class storage split (`.ddx/exec-runs/` vs `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`) collapses into one substrate.
12	
13	These threads converge: **generating an artifact is a layer-1 agent run** with `produces_artifact` metadata.
14	
15	## Decisions (resolved through review)
16	
17	- **Artifact authority:** identity present → artifact. No "managed asset" second-class concept.
18	- **FEAT-019 fate:** child of FEAT-010 (not peer). Contracts to evaluation UX; workflow shapes (comparison, replay, benchmark) move to skills library.
19	- **Server posture:** 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP for all DDx state, sequenced by impact. Writes case-by-case as workflows demand. Only `artifactRegenerate` write surface added in this plan.
20	- **Generated artifacts:** committed to git unconditionally. Size guard (50MB warn / 100MB hard error) with actionable git-lfs guidance; no auto-init / auto-`.gitattributes`.
21	- **CONTRACT-003:** amendments allowed if FEAT-006 work surfaces gaps. The mountable Cobra root export (below) is a load-bearing audit item.
22	- **Run substrate:** single on-disk shape; layer-2 and layer-3 add metadata. `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse.
23	- **No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.** Comparison, replay, benchmark, etc. are skill compositions, never enshrined in Go core or specs.
24	- **Loop ownership sharpened:** DDx owns mechanical queue drain (`ddx work`); content-aware supervisory decisions (e.g., "comparison failed → enqueue reconciliation beads") remain plugin/HELIX territory.
25	- **CLI verbs:** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` promoted to top-level. Hard-deprecate `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop` (exit non-zero with bare redirect message — no aliases, no grace period).
26	- **Generate-artifact layer placement:** pure-return generators (agent returns bytes/text, DDx writes file) are layer-1. Repo-editing generators are layer-2 with bead-less worktree.
27	- **Quorum dispatch:** moves to skill composition. `--quorum` flag goes away; replaced by `compare-prompts` skill.
28	- **`ddx agent` passthrough is structural:** DDx imports the upstream agent module's Cobra root and mounts it under `ddx agent`. Notionally `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))`. DDx does not enumerate per-subcommand wrappers; the entire upstream tree is the source of truth.
29	
30	## Three-layer architecture
31	
32	| Layer | CLI | Inputs | Outputs / side effects | Owns |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| 1 | `ddx run` | prompt + agent config | structured output, side effects, run metadata (tokens, model, duration, exit) | invocation atom; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per CONTRACT-003 |
35	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | bead id, base revision | new worktree state + agent-run evidence; merge or preserve | worktree start/end capture; bead → prompt resolution; side-effect bundling |
36	| 3 | `ddx work` | bead queue, stop conditions | sequence of `ddx try` records + loop-level record (drained / blocked / deferred) | mechanical queue drain; no-progress detection |
37	
38	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`. `ddx work` iterates `ddx try`. One on-disk substrate; layer metadata distinguishes records.
39	
40	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 if the generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata.
41	
42	## Frame artifact updates
43	
44	### docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
45	- Thesis: "documents AI agents consume" → "artifacts agents produce and consume."
46	- Artifact-management bullet: non-document media + generators.
47	- One sentence on producer/consumer space (no separate matrix).
48	- New Design-Philosophy subsection: **Three-layer run architecture** — `run` / `try` / `work` named as DDx's loop ownership.
49	- KVPs: "Multi-media artifact graph"; "Three-layer run architecture (run/try/work)"; note invocation is upstream.
50	- Principle #1: "Artifacts are the product (documents primary)."
51	
52	### docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
53	- Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate.
54	- Problem: add "no provenance for generated artifacts."
55	- Goals: reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add "100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP."
56	- Non-goals: sharpen loop wording (mechanical drain in scope; supervisory content-aware decisions out). Add: "DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers."
57	- FEAT-001/005/006/007/008/010/019/021 blurbs updated.
58	
59	### FEAT-001-cli.md
60	- Promote `run` / `try` / `work` to top-level.
61	- `ddx agent` mounts upstream Cobra root structurally; no DDx-defined leaf subcommands beneath it.
62	- Hard-deprecation handlers for `ddx agent {run, execute-bead, execute-loop}`.
63	- New namespaces: `ddx runs` (cross-layer evidence introspection), `ddx tries` (layer-2 specifically), `ddx work workers` (layer-3 worker management).
64	- Remove FEAT-001:92 backward-compatibility clause for `ddx agent execute-loop`.
65	
66	### FEAT-005-artifacts.md
67	- Identity broadens to non-markdown via sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
68	- Add `media_type` and `generated_by` fields.
69	- Authority rule documented: identity present → artifact.
70	- Terminology cleanup ("identity," not "ddx: identity").
71	
72	### FEAT-006-agent-service.md
73	- Tighten to layer-1 consumer-side wrapper that powers `ddx run`.
74	- `ddx agent` is structural passthrough (mount upstream Cobra root).
75	- Non-bead Profile/Permissions selection (artifact-keyed path).
76	- Migration table: what stays in FEAT-006 (CONTRACT-003 boundary, profile/permissions), what moves to FEAT-010 (worktree, merge/preserve, evidence bundles, queue drain), what moves to FEAT-001 (CLI surface).
77	- Session-log boundary clarified: DDx owns the envelope/pointer in evidence bundles; upstream owns inner log shape.
78	
79	### FEAT-010-executions.md (substantial refactor)
80	- Three-layer architecture explicit and load-bearing.
81	- Substrate unification: one record shape; layer metadata.
82	- Migrate `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` to one layout.
83	- Specify `ddx work` no-progress / stop conditions.
84	- Narrow read-only amendment for `artifactRegenerate` only.
85	- No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.
86	
87	### FEAT-007-doc-graph.md
88	- Sidecar scanner refactor; `media_type`; `generated_by` edge with separate staleness rule (provenance, not authority — does not cascade like `depends_on`); version bump on Complete-status feature.
89	- 100% read endpoints for graph state, generated-artifact metadata, sidecar content.
90	
91	### FEAT-008-web-ui.md
92	- Media-type-aware rendering (markdown, mermaid SVG, excalidraw embed, image inline, PDF embed).
93	- Regenerate action wired to `artifactRegenerate` mutation; reconcile with existing FEAT-008:391 weak Re-run requirement.
94	- Layer-aware run views (`work` → `try` → `run` drill-down).
95	
96	### FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md
97	- Contract to evaluation UX (comparison views, grading rubric storage and display, benchmark result aggregation).
98	- Workflow shapes → skills library.
99	- Remove peer-to-FEAT-010 assertions (FEAT-019:40, :404).
100	
101	### FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md
102	- Layer-aware run-history routes against unified substrate.
103	
104	### FEAT-014, FEAT-013
105	- One-sentence touches: cost attribution and concurrency for generate-artifact.
106	
107	## `ddx agent` subcommand fates
108	
109	`ddx agent` becomes a structural mount of the upstream agent CLI Cobra root. Per-file fate in DDx:
110	
111	**Delete with prejudice (upstream owns these via mounted subtree):**
112	- `agent_list.go`
113	- `agent_capabilities.go`
114	- `agent_doctor.go`
115	- `agent_check.go`
116	- `agent_models.go`
117	- `agent_providers.go`
118	- `agent_route_status.go`
119	- `agent_catalog.go`
120	- `agent_reindex.go`
121	- `agent_benchmark.go` (replaced by `benchmark-suite` skill)
122	- `agent_replay.go` (replaced by `replay-bead` skill)
123	
124	**Move to other DDx namespaces (DDx-only concerns):**
125	- `agent_executions.go` → `tries_*.go`
126	- `agent_log.go` → `runs_log.go`
127	- `agent_metrics.go` → `runs_metrics.go`
128	- `agent_usage.go` → folded into `runs_metrics.go`
129	- `agent_workers.go` → `work_workers.go`
130	
131	**DDx-side overrides on the mounted subtree:**
132	- Deprecation handlers for `agent run` / `agent execute-bead` / `agent execute-loop`.
133	- `agent condense` (DDx-specific output filter — keep as override or move upstream during impl pass).
134	
135	**`agent_cmd.go` itself thins to:** mount upstream Cobra root + register DDx overrides.
136	
137	## Skill library (DDx-shipped workflows)
138	
139	Per FEAT-011's path model: source at `skills/ddx/`, embedded at `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`, init-time copies to `.claude/skills/ddx/`, `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.ddx/skills/ddx/`.
140	
141	- `compare-prompts` — N-arm dispatch + aggregation (replaces `--quorum` flag and `agent benchmark`)
142	- `replay-bead` — re-run with altered conditions, baseline diff (replaces `agent replay`)
143	- `benchmark-suite` — compare across prompt matrix
144	- `adversarial-review`
145	- `effort-estimate`
146	- `bead-breakdown`
147	
148	## Bead breakdown
149	
150	**Frame updates (sequential where dependencies require):**
151	
152	1. Update product-vision.md (+ three-layer subsection)
153	2. Update prd.md (+ three-layer goal, sharpened loop non-goal, no-catalog non-goal)
154	3. Update FEAT-001 (CLI surface refactor; remove backcompat clause; new namespaces) — **hard predecessor for #6, #7**
155	4. Update FEAT-005 (multi-media identity refactor)
156	5. CONTRACT-003 audit — confirm `ddx-agent` exports a mountable Cobra root and other gaps
157	6. *(Conditional)* Open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent` if #5 surfaces gaps
158	7. Update FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer; structural passthrough; non-bead Profile selection; migration table; session-log envelope boundary)
159	8. Refactor FEAT-010 (three-layer architecture; substrate unification; on-disk migration; no-progress stop conditions; narrow `artifactRegenerate` amendment)
160	9. Update FEAT-007 (sidecar refactor; media_type; generated_by; separate staleness; version bump)
161	10. Contract FEAT-019 to evaluation UX
162	11. Update FEAT-008 (media rendering, regenerate action, layer-aware run views)
163	12. Update FEAT-021 (layer-aware run-history routes)
164	13. Update FEAT-014 (cost attribution for generate-artifact)
165	14. Update FEAT-013 (concurrency note for regenerate)
166	15. **Repo-wide audit & verb migration** — atomic with #3:
167	    - Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands (list above).
168	    - Migrate DDx-only run/evidence subcommands to `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`.
169	    - Register DDx overrides on the mounted subtree (deprecations + condense).
170	    - Delete `agent_reindex.go` outright.
171	    - Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to new verbs.
172	
173	**Design artifacts (concurrent with frame):**
174	
175	16. Architecture: artifact identity schema (concurrent with #4)
176	17. Architecture: graph schema with media_type + generated_by + separate staleness (concurrent with #9)
177	18. Architecture: three-layer run substrate — record shape, layer metadata, on-disk layout, migration, `ddx work` stop conditions (concurrent with #8)
178	19. Sidecar `.ddx.yaml` schema design
179	
180	**Read-coverage thread:**
181	
182	20. Read-coverage audit — enumerate every CLI-visible read; map to HTTP/MCP coverage; identify gaps
183	21. Per-gap beads — sequenced by user-visibility impact, land case-by-case (this is a pointer to ongoing work, not a single bead)
184	
185	**Skill library:**
186	
187	22. `compare-prompts` skill
188	23. `replay-bead` skill
189	24. `benchmark-suite` skill
190	25. `adversarial-review` skill
191	26. `effort-estimate` skill
192	27. `bead-breakdown` skill
193	
194	**Implementation (P2, after frame & design):**
195	
196	- CLI: `ddx run` / `ddx try` top-level commands; mount upstream agent Cobra root; deprecation handlers
197	- CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)
198	- CLI: `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`, `ddx artifact list --media-type <t>`
199	- Storage: sidecar reader/writer; size-guard threshold/warning/tests
200	- Graph: `generated_by` edges + provenance staleness rule
201	- Run framework: refactor to one substrate per #18; generator metadata; `ddx work` no-progress detection
202	- Server: `artifactRegenerate` write endpoint
203	- Server UI: media-type renderers; regenerate action; layer-aware run history
204	- Migration: passive `media_type` only (read-time inference from extension); on-disk run-record migration per #18
205	
206	## Constraints
207	
208	- Beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — old verbs are referenced in scripts/library/personas/init help text/CI; an isolated #3 break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced this plan.
209	- Bead #5 (CONTRACT-003 audit) gates #7 (FEAT-006). #6 conditional.
210	- Architecture sketches (#16–#19) run concurrent with their corresponding frame beads, not after — keeps frame/design from drifting.
211	- Read-coverage epic (#20–#21) is independent and not gated on this plan.
212	
213	## Refinement provenance
214	
215	Refined through 3 rounds of adversarial review (codex via `ddx agent run --harness codex` + fresh-eyes general-purpose subagent in parallel) plus user-resolved questions Q1–Q12. Key user decisions: artifact authority (Q1), FEAT-019 child-of-010 (Q2), reads-matter-writes-case-by-case (Q3), check-in unconditionally (Q4), CONTRACT-003 amendments allowed (Q5), substrate unification (Q6), no run-type catalog (Q7), no v1-gate framing (Q8), FEAT-019 contracts (Q9), generate-artifact split layer-1/layer-2 (Q10), quorum-as-skill (Q11), structural passthrough not per-command (Q12 follow-up).

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: TD-025
4	  depends_on:
5	    - FEAT-005
6	    - FEAT-007
7	---
8	# Technical Design: Sidecar `.ddx.yaml` Schema
9	
10	## Purpose
11	
12	This design specifies the sidecar file format that extends DDx artifact identity
13	to non-markdown files (images, diagrams, PDFs, binary exports, plain-text
14	prompts, etc.). It covers three decisions the scanner and validator must agree
15	on:
16	
17	1. **Pairing rule** — how a sidecar file maps to its paired asset.
18	2. **Conflict rule** — what happens when a file could claim identity via both
19	   embedded frontmatter and a sidecar.
20	3. **Orphan-check participation** — how sidecar-identified artifacts are counted
21	   in `ddx doc validate`.
22	
23	## Pairing Rule
24	
25	A sidecar is a YAML file whose name is the **full filename of the paired asset
26	with `.ddx.yaml` appended**:
27	
28	```
29	foo.png          ←→  foo.png.ddx.yaml
30	diagram.excalidraw ←→ diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml
31	hero.svg         ←→  hero.svg.ddx.yaml
32	arch.pdf         ←→  arch.pdf.ddx.yaml
33	system-prompt.txt ←→ system-prompt.txt.ddx.yaml
34	```
35	
36	Rules:
37	
38	- The sidecar and its paired asset **must reside in the same directory**.
39	  A `foo.png.ddx.yaml` in a subdirectory does **not** pair with a `foo.png`
40	  in the parent directory, even if the paths are otherwise nearby.
41	- Sidecar filenames are **case-sensitive** on case-sensitive filesystems. The
42	  scanner must match exactly and must not normalize case.
43	- A `.ddx.yaml` file with no matching asset alongside it is a dangling sidecar.
44	  `ddx doc validate` reports it as an error (not just a warning), because it
45	  implies either the asset was deleted or the sidecar was incorrectly named.
46	
47	### Why append, not replace
48	
49	Appending `.ddx.yaml` to the full filename (including any existing extension)
50	preserves the original filename as an unambiguous prefix. Replacing the last
51	extension (`foo.png` → `foo.ddx.yaml`) creates ambiguity: a file named
52	`foo.ddx.yaml` alongside a `foo.png` would be misread as the sidecar of a
53	hypothetical `foo.ddx` asset, and files without extensions (`Makefile`,
54	`Dockerfile`) would produce bare `.ddx.yaml` sidecar names that glob patterns
55	cannot reliably distinguish from project-level config files.
56	
57	### Glob pattern for discovery
58	
59	The scanner uses the glob `**/*.ddx.yaml` to collect all sidecar candidates,
60	then strips the `.ddx.yaml` suffix to locate the expected paired asset.
61	
62	## Sidecar File Schema
63	
64	A sidecar is a YAML document (no frontmatter delimiters — it is pure YAML, not
65	markdown). The top-level key is `ddx:`, matching the frontmatter namespace used
66	in markdown artifacts.
67	
68	### Minimal example
69	
70	```yaml
71	ddx:
72	  id: DIAG-001
73	```
74	
75	### Full example
76	
77	```yaml
78	ddx:
79	  id: DIAG-001
80	  media_type: image/png
81	  depends_on:
82	    - FEAT-005
83	    - FEAT-007
84	  generated_by: SD-011
85	  inputs:
86	    - node:FEAT-005
87	  parking_lot: false
88	```
89	
90	### Field reference
91	
92	| Field | Type | Required | Description |
93	|-------|------|----------|-------------|
94	| `id` | string | **yes** | Artifact ID. Same prefix conventions as markdown artifacts (e.g., `DIAG-001`, `HERO-003`). Must be unique across the project graph. |
95	| `media_type` | string | no | IANA media type of the paired asset (e.g., `image/png`, `image/svg+xml`, `application/pdf`). When absent, DDx infers from the file extension. Explicit value overrides inference. |
96	| `depends_on` | []string | no | IDs of artifacts this one depends on. Creates `depends_on` edges in the document graph. Staleness cascades from these edges as with any artifact. |
97	| `generated_by` | string | no | ID of the artifact (typically a spec or design) that describes how this asset is produced. Creates a `generated_by` edge, which is **provenance-only** — it does not trigger staleness cascade (see Staleness below). |
98	| `inputs` | []string | no | Input selectors for prompt context resolution. Same selector syntax as markdown frontmatter. |
99	| `review` | object | no | Staleness tracking metadata (managed by `ddx doc stamp`). Same sub-fields as markdown: `self_hash`, `deps`, `reviewed_at`. The hash covers the paired asset bytes, not the sidecar YAML. |
100	| `parking_lot` | bool | no | When `true`, skip in staleness checks. Same semantics as markdown. |
101	
102	Unknown fields within `ddx:` are preserved on round-trip.
103	
104	### `generated_by` vs `depends_on` staleness semantics
105	
106	| Edge type | Triggers staleness cascade? | Meaning |
107	|-----------|----------------------------|---------|
108	| `depends_on` | **Yes** | This artifact is governed by the upstream. If the upstream changes, this artifact may need updating. |
109	| `generated_by` | **No** | Provenance only. Records which spec or design describes the generator. Changing the generator spec does not automatically make the asset stale — regeneration is triggered explicitly via `ddx artifact regenerate`. |
110	
111	This distinction mirrors the plan decision: "`generated_by` edge with separate
112	staleness rule (provenance, not authority — does not cascade like `depends_on`)."
113	
114	## Conflict Rule
115	
116	**Markdown files use embedded frontmatter exclusively. Non-markdown files use
117	sidecars exclusively. Mixing the two on a single asset is a validation error.**
118	
119	The concrete conflict cases and their resolution:
120	
121	| Situation | Outcome |
122	|-----------|---------|
123	| Markdown file with `ddx:` frontmatter; no sidecar alongside it | Normal markdown artifact. |
124	| Non-markdown file with a sidecar; no embedded YAML possible | Normal sidecar artifact. |
125	| Markdown file (`*.md`) with a `*.md.ddx.yaml` sidecar alongside it | **Validation error.** `ddx doc validate` reports: `<path>.md.ddx.yaml: sidecar alongside markdown file '<path>.md' — markdown artifacts declare identity in frontmatter; remove the sidecar`. The sidecar is ignored for graph construction to avoid duplicate IDs. |
126	| Two sidecars claim the same `id` as an existing markdown artifact | **Validation error.** Duplicate-ID check fires identically to the case where two markdown files share an ID. |
127	| A sidecar's `id` duplicates another sidecar's `id` | **Validation error.** Same duplicate-ID check. |
128	
129	The prohibition on markdown+sidecar combination is strict because DDx's authority
130	rule is "identity present → artifact." Allowing both sources on a markdown file
131	would require a precedence rule, invite inconsistency, and add scanner complexity
132	for zero real-world benefit — markdown files can always carry their own identity
133	in frontmatter.
134	
135	## Orphan-Check Participation
136	
137	Sidecar-identified artifacts participate in `ddx doc validate` orphan detection
138	**identically to markdown artifacts**. No special treatment is applied.
139	
140	An artifact is an orphan when it has no incoming edges (nothing depends on it)
141	**and** no outgoing edges (it depends on nothing). Sidecar artifacts with at
142	least one `depends_on` or one reverse-dependency edge from another artifact are
143	not orphaned.
144	
145	### Common non-orphan patterns for sidecar artifacts
146	
147	```
148	FEAT-005 ──depends_on──▶ DIAG-001   (FEAT-005 depends on the diagram)
149	DIAG-001 ──depends_on──▶ FEAT-007   (diagram depends on the doc-graph spec)
150	DIAG-001 ──generated_by─▶ SD-011    (provenance only — not an authority edge)
151	```
152	
153	A `generated_by` edge **does count** toward orphan suppression: if DIAG-001
154	has only a `generated_by` link to SD-011, it is not considered orphaned, because
155	the provenance edge establishes that the asset is intentional and traceable.
156	
157	### Dangling sidecar rule (distinct from orphan)
158	
159	A `.ddx.yaml` file with no paired asset is **not** an orphan — it is a dangling
160	sidecar. These are reported separately from graph orphans:
161	
162	```
163	ERROR: foo.png.ddx.yaml: sidecar has no paired asset 'foo.png' in the same directory
164	```
165	
166	Dangling sidecars are hard errors (not warnings) because they indicate either
167	an accidental deletion or a copy-paste naming mistake, and they leave an artifact
168	ID in the graph that resolves to no file.
169	
170	## Scanner Integration
171	
172	The FEAT-007 scanner refactor must handle sidecars in the following order:
173	
174	1. Glob `**/*.ddx.yaml` → collect sidecar candidates.
175	2. For each candidate, strip `.ddx.yaml` suffix → derive expected paired-asset path.
176	3. Check: does a file exist at that path in the same directory?
177	   - No → record dangling sidecar error; skip adding to graph.
178	   - Yes, and the paired asset is a `.md` file → record markdown+sidecar conflict error; skip.
179	   - Yes, and paired asset is non-markdown → parse YAML, extract `ddx:` block, add artifact to graph.
180	4. Continue normal markdown frontmatter scan for all `*.md` files.
181	5. Build unified graph from both sources. Apply duplicate-ID detection across both.
182	
183	The scanner must not attempt to parse binary assets. It only reads the `.ddx.yaml`
184	sidecar file; the paired asset's bytes are only accessed when computing
185	`review.self_hash` during `ddx doc stamp`.
186	
187	## Content Hashing for `ddx doc stamp`
188	
189	When `ddx doc stamp` processes a sidecar artifact:
190	
191	- `review.self_hash` is the SHA-256 of the **paired asset bytes** (not the sidecar YAML).
192	- The sidecar YAML is rewritten in-place with the updated `review` block.
193	- The hash covers the complete byte sequence of the paired asset as it exists on disk; no normalization is applied (images and binary files have no canonical form).
194	
195	## Validation Summary
196	
197	`ddx doc validate` checks on sidecar artifacts:
198	
199	| Check | Severity | Message pattern |
200	|-------|----------|-----------------|
201	| Dangling sidecar (no paired asset) | error | `<sidecar>: sidecar has no paired asset '<asset>' in the same directory` |
202	| Sidecar alongside a markdown file | error | `<sidecar>: sidecar alongside markdown file '<md>' — use frontmatter instead` |
203	| Duplicate ID (sidecar vs. any artifact) | error | `duplicate id '<id>': found in '<path-1>' and '<path-2>'` |
204	| Sidecar with no `ddx.id` field | error | `<sidecar>: missing required field ddx.id` |
205	| Sidecar artifact with no graph edges (orphan) | warning | `<id>: artifact has no edges — add depends_on or ensure another artifact depends on it` |
206	| `depends_on` references unknown ID | warning | `<id>: unknown dependency '<ref-id>'` |
207	| `generated_by` references unknown ID | warning | `<id>: unknown generated_by target '<ref-id>'` |
208

data.text: The work was already completed in commit `cda66189`, which is an ancestor of HEAD. TD-025 fully addresses all three contract items from the bead description. The previous review's "REVIEW:BLOCK" appears to have inspected only the execution metadata diff rather than the merged file.

input.content:
Bead ddx-24a9286f — sidecar .ddx.yaml schema design — is already complete.

What is done
------------
The design document exists at:
  docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-025-sidecar-ddx-yaml-schema.md

It was authored on a previous attempt and merged via commit cda66189
("docs: sidecar .ddx.yaml schema design (TD-025) [ddx-24a9286f]"), which is
an ancestor of the current base revision f21bd2b6 (verified with
`git merge-base --is-ancestor cda66189 HEAD`). The file is tracked
(`git ls-files` confirms) and present in the worktree.

TD-025 covers all three items called out in the bead description:

1. Pairing rule
   Section "Pairing Rule" specifies append-not-replace
   (foo.png ↔ foo.png.ddx.yaml, diagram.excalidraw ↔
   diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml). Same-directory requirement,
   case-sensitivity, glob discovery (`**/*.ddx.yaml`), and rationale
   for append-vs-replace are all documented.

2. Conflict rule (frontmatter + sidecar)
   Section "Conflict Rule" sets the strict policy: markdown uses
   embedded frontmatter exclusively, non-markdown uses sidecars
   exclusively. A `*.md.ddx.yaml` sidecar alongside a `*.md` file is a
   validation error; sidecar is ignored to avoid duplicate IDs. Full
   conflict table with outcomes is provided.

3. Orphan-check participation in `ddx doc validate`
   Section "Orphan-Check Participation" specifies parity with markdown
   artifacts, the role of `generated_by` edges in suppressing orphan
   warnings (provenance-only but does count for orphan suppression),
   and the distinct "dangling sidecar" hard-error rule for sidecars
   without paired assets. Validation summary table at the end
   enumerates every check and severity.

What is blocking
----------------
Nothing is blocking; there is no further work for this bead. The
prior review note ("REVIEW:BLOCK — diff contains only execution
manifest/result metadata; the actual sidecar schema design document
referenced as cda66189 in result_rev is not part of the changed
files") appears to have inspected the execution-metadata diff rather
than the merged source tree. The artifact named in result_rev is
present in the repository at HEAD.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
A follow-up attempt should not regenerate or rewrite TD-025 — that
would churn already-approved design content and risk diverging from
the consumers of the schema (FEAT-005 / FEAT-007 scanner refactor in
later beads). If the reviewer believes specific gaps remain, those
gaps should be enumerated as concrete acceptance items
(e.g., "specify behavior when sidecar YAML is malformed", "define
sidecar schema-version field") so the next attempt has a precise
delta to address. Re-queuing on the same description will produce
the same conclusion.

data.text:
The bead's deliverable — `docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-025-sidecar-ddx-yaml-schema.md` — already exists in the tree (committed as `cda66189`, ancestor of HEAD) and covers all three items in the description: pairing rule (append `.ddx.yaml`, same-directory), conflict rule (markdown uses frontmatter, non-markdown uses sidecar; mixing is a validation error), and orphan-check participation (parity with markdown artifacts, plus a separate dangling-sidecar hard error).

Wrote `.ddx/executions/20260430T012006-fd9cefb2/no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining why this attempt makes no commit. The prior `REVIEW:BLOCK` note appears to have read execution metadata instead of the merged source.
